verses.text: Haman is promoted above all the princes, and all bow to him — but Mordecai will not. Where conscience forbids, he stands unmoved. The believer gives honour where due, yet will not bend where bowing would betray his God.

Haman is promoted above all the princes, and all bow to him — but Mordecai will not. Where conscience forbids, he stands unmoved. The believer gives honour where due, yet will not bend where bowing would betray his God.
Pressed daily, Mordecai explains that he is a Jew, and the matter is reported to Haman. He does not hide his faith though it brings danger. Loyalty to God is owned openly, whatever it may cost.
Pressed daily, Mordecai explains that he is a Jew, and the matter is reported to Haman. He does not hide his faith though it brings danger. Loyalty to God is owned openly, whatever it may cost.

Filled with rage, Haman scorns to touch Mordecai alone and plots to destroy every Jew in the empire. Wounded pride swells into murderous hatred. Sin unchecked grows monstrous, turning a personal slight into a design against a whole people.

Filled with rage, Haman scorns to touch Mordecai alone and plots to destroy every Jew in the empire. Wounded pride swells into murderous hatred. Sin unchecked grows monstrous, turning a personal slight into a design against a whole people.

Haman casts Pur, the lot, to choose the day of slaughter. He trusts chance to time his cruelty. Yet 'the lot is cast into the lap, but the whole disposing is of the LORD,' who overrules even the gambler's dice.

Haman tells the king of a scattered people with different laws, and offers silver to have them destroyed. Truth is twisted into a deadly slander. The enemy of God's people paints their distinctness as danger, masking malice as concern for the realm.

Haman tells the king of a scattered people with different laws, and offers silver to have them destroyed. Truth is twisted into a deadly slander. The enemy of God's people paints their distinctness as danger, masking malice as concern for the realm.

The king carelessly hands Haman his ring and the people to do with as he pleases. A whole nation is signed away over wine. Power without conscience can doom multitudes with a careless word, but God still reigns above the throne.

The king carelessly hands Haman his ring and the people to do with as he pleases. A whole nation is signed away over wine. Power without conscience can doom multitudes with a careless word, but God still reigns above the throne.

The decree is written and sped to every province — destroy all the Jews on one day — while king and Haman sit down to drink, and the city is bewildered. Cruelty feasts while the innocent tremble. Yet the very date set for ruin God has already marked for deliverance.
